strengths: Provides detailed examples of applying ATL skills (active listening, collaboration, problem-solving) with evidence of their effectiveness
Demonstrates adaptability by transferring prior mentoring experience to support the menteeâs growth
Highlights strong social ATL skills through collaborative freestyle drawings
weaknesses: Evidence table is empty and needs matching descriptions for each success criterion
Final drawings are not directly linked back to success criteriaâform, aesthetics, function and audience need explicit alignment
Discussion of thinking skills is insightful but lacks concrete evidence (for example, metrics or student confidence levels)
Volunteer badge and hours sheet are presented but not analyzed for their impact on ATL skill development
Baseline sketches are not quantified to show specific learning gains
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student effectively illustrates how ATL skills guided their mentoring approach and peer collaboration. To deepen this analysis, each piece of evidence must be clearly tied to a success criterion and supported with measurable data. Reflecting on volunteer records and quantifying improvements in sketches will demonstrate the real impact of chosen strategies and strengthen the linkage between actions and outcomes.

strengths: Establishes a compelling personal connection to the learning goal, showing genuine motivation and clear context
Identifies a comprehensive array of resources (materials, tools, research sources), demonstrating thorough preparation
weaknesses: Intended product description is incomplete and ends mid-sentence, leaving ambiguity about what success looks like
Plan of action lacks full sequencingâonly one milestone date is listed and contingency plans are absent
Success criteria are detailed but not measurable; no quantitative targets to track progress objectively
Overview of progress is engaging but not explicitly linked to success criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student shows real enthusiasm and has gathered a wide range of resources, which lays a strong foundation. To strengthen this section, the intended product must be fully articulated, and the plan must include all key milestones, dates, resources and contingency measures. Adding quantifiable success indicators and explicitly connecting the overview to those criteria will ensure the plan is both clear and feasible.

strengths: Overall reflection contains insightful observations about the learning experience
weaknesses: Reflection lacks a criterion-by-criterion evaluation paired with concrete examples
Product evaluation table is incomplete and missing entries aligned to each success criterion
Evaluation matrix is redundant and needs consolidation for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflective narrative shows good self-awareness, but it requires more structure and completeness. A thorough, criterion-by-criterion review with specific evidence will provide clear insights into achievement and areas for growth. Completing the evaluation table and streamlining the matrix will make the reflection more focused and actionable.

annotations.comment: Consider specifying software or online platforms under âComputerâ to ensure you have the right digital tools for tutorials and sketching references.
You provide detailed examples of applying ATL skillsâactive listening, collaboration and problem-solvingâto build rapport and adapt your mentoring approach effectively.
Build in contingency plans for missed Tuesdaysâthis will help you maintain consistent progress if sessions need rescheduling.
You establish a compelling personal connection to your learning goal, clearly explaining how your experience shadowing your mother shaped your motivation and defined your mentorship objective.
The intended product description ends mid-sentence. Complete this section to fully articulate what success looks like and ensure feasibility.
Your plan of action lists only one date. Expand this table with all key milestones, dates and required resources to show clear sequencing and feasibility.
Your Evidence table is empty. Populate it with succinct descriptions matching each piece of evidence to a specific success criterion.
Your opening overview of progress is engagingâconsider linking it explicitly to your success criteria to orient the reader to what you will measure.
The success criteria are detailed but lack quantifiable targets. Add measurable indicators (e.g., number of drawings completed or accuracy percentages) to track progress objectively.

Your discussion of thinking skills is insightful; strengthen it by citing specific evidence of how these strategies improved the final productâs quality (e.g., symmetry metrics or student confidence levels).
Link each final drawing image directly back to your success criteria (form, aesthetics, function, audience) to demonstrate how the product meets those benchmarks.
Youâve identified a comprehensive set of resourcesâmaterials, tools and research sourcesâto support your mentoring sessions, demonstrating thorough preparation.
Your example of transferring pre-existing mentoring experience to support your mentee underlines strong adaptability and shows how skills translate across contexts.
The volunteer badge image is a solid record of commitment; reference it in your analysis by explaining how this presence supported development of specific ATL skills.
Your volunteer hours sheet documents engagement wellâconsider reflecting on how consistent attendance strengthened your time-management and planning skills.
Parisâs initial sketches are valuable baseline evidenceâquantify specific improvements (e.g., reduction in proportion errors) to show clear learning gains.
Your collaborative freestyle drawings highlight effective peer collaboration and co-construction of ideas, showcasing strong social ATL skills.
Your overall reflection is insightful but would benefit from a criterion-by-criterion evaluation, pairing each success criterion with concrete examples of achievement.
The product evaluation table is truncated and lacks complete entriesâensure strengths and development areas are fully articulated and aligned to each criterion.
The matrix on page 12 repeats narrativeâstreamline by consolidating evaluation points and avoiding redundancy for a clearer reflection.
